reasoning: The 105% gain is real and justified by strong execution — profit growth, customer acquisition, dividend initiation all validate the thesis. But the bear's point about sector beta is worth noting: Israeli insurance had a monster 2025 and some mean-reversion risk exists. At ~4-6% portfolio weight, the position is appropriately sized. The P/E at 11x is still cheap relative to growth. I'll hold here — the company is executing well and the macro environment (BoI cuts, strong shekel, TASE ATH) remains supportive. No need to take profits when the thesis is intact. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Reduce if P/E expands above 18x or if quarterly profit growth decelerates below 20%. Add only on a pullback toward ₪1,200-1,300 (200d MA zone) with continued profit growth.
entryConditions: Add only on a pullback toward ₪1,200-1,300 (200d MA zone) with continued profit growth.
exitConditions: Reduce if P/E expands above 18x or if quarterly profit growth decelerates below 20%

POSITION SIZE: ~19,236 ILS (~4-6% of portfolio)
CONFIDENCE: medium (execution strong, but sector euphoria warrants caution)

bullCase: LBRA is a fast-growing, profitable digital insurer — Q3 profit +85% YoY, P/E ~11x (cheap for growth), 300K+ customers, just initiated ₪10M dividend. Israeli insurance sector rallied 134% in 2025 with TASE at all-time highs. BoI cutting rates to 4.0%, shekel strong. The digital-first model is disrupting legacy Israeli insurers and the 105% gain is justified by fundamentals, not speculation.
bearCase: LBRA has been swept up in historic sector-wide euphoria — the 105% gain reflects momentum and sector beta more than company-specific moat. Stock is priced for perfection with no margin of safety if growth decelerates. 19% correction from ATH ₪1,985 shows vulnerability. Small-cap liquidity risk — thin trading volume. No formal analyst coverage limits price discovery. Sector rally may be late-cycle.
